tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Cancer Survivor Conquers 100-Mile Mountain Race One Year After Surgery
stories.summary: Christina Klayko, a two-time cancer survivor, completes the grueling Western States Endurance Run just 12 months after major surgery and radiation—a stunning testament to human resilience.

stories.headline: Goalkeeper Who Sold His Jersey to Save His Son Becomes World Cup Hero
stories.summary: Orlando Gill, who sold his treasured jerseys four years ago to pay for newborn son Lautaro Daniel's life-saving surgery, became Paraguay's World Cup hero, denying Germany twice in a dramatic penalty shootout.

stories.headline: Young Girl's Lemonade Stand Honors Lost Community Hero, Feeds the Hungry
stories.summary: Ava set up a lemonade stand to raise funds for her late hero Sam Saberi's free meal program, keeping his legacy of generosity alive in her neighborhood.

stories.headline: From Ashes of Loss, a Garden Blooms—and Feeds a Neighborhood
stories.summary: After losing her home to fire, Yvette Leno transformed grief into Beauty's Community Garden, freely sharing fresh produce and her grandmother's spirit of generosity with Independence Heights.

stories.headline: Police Officer Becomes Angel in the Heat, Buying Water for Vulnerable Neighbors
stories.summary: Officer Evan Howard personally purchases cases of water for homeless and vulnerable neighbors during extreme heat, enlisting his grocery store and community to help.

stories.headline: Special Olympics Athletes Return Home as Champions, Break National Records
stories.summary: Gabriella Baker, 21-year-old Dothan athlete, set a new Special Olympics USA Games record in the 800-meter run with a time of 2:33.88, honoring her family's running legacy.

stories.headline: Ronaldo Honors Fallen Teammate, Scores World Cup Knockout Goal in Emotional Victory
stories.summary: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo scored his first World Cup knockout goal and wore late teammate Diogo Jota's No. 21 jersey after Portugal's 2-1 victory, on the first anniversary of Jota's death.

stories.headline: Rare Butterflies Return Home: 300 Mitchell's Satyrs Reintroduced to Restored Wetland
stories.summary: After 20 years of work, natural resource specialist Michele Richards and her team released 300 endangered Mitchell's satyr butterflies into Fort Custer's restored wetland, a pinnacle moment for military conservation.

stories.headline: Endangered Crayfish Get a Second Chance: Britain's First New Wild Population Established
stories.summary: Wildwood Trust released 70 white-clawed crayfish into a Devon nature reserve, establishing Britain's first new wild population of this endangered species through captive breeding from the River Creedy-Yeo.

stories.headline: Korean Engineers Grow Artificial Pancreas Cells That Nearly Match Healthy Human Insulin
stories.summary: POSTECH researchers used 3D printing to create artificial pancreas cells with electrical stimulation, nearly matching the insulin-secreting capacity of healthy human cells—a breakthrough for type 1 diabetes treatment.
